qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: API basic-auth personal access token issuance bypasses two-factor authentication
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a missing 2FA re-check during Basic-auth PAT issuance in internal/context/auth.go and internal/route/api/v1/user/app.go, not the advisory's symlink-based .git/config write through UpdateRepoFile from the API contents route.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable code/root cause: updating .git/config through the API contents update path by writing through a repository symlink that reaches UpdateRepoFile.
evidence: The advisory describes PUT /api/v1/repos/:owner/:repo/contents/* passing a cleaned attacker-controlled path into UpdateRepoFile, so a committed symlink such as link -> .git/config can still update Git metadata and lead to RCE.

The only final qualifying finding, api_pat_2fa_bypass, is rooted in internal/context/auth.go returning a user after Basic-auth password verification without checking db.TwoFactors.IsEnabled, and internal/route/api/v1/user/app.go then minting a PAT for that user.
That qualifying finding does not involve repository file mutation, symlink dereference, .git/config, or the API contents write path named in the advisory.

A filesystem-adjacent finding exists only as windows_git_alias_write, but it is non_qualifying_vuln and describes a different Windows trailing-space .git alias mechanism rather than the advisory's symlink-to-.git/config bug.
